phase.name: Python SDK Feature Parity
phase.exit_criteria: All 6 missing Client control methods are implemented, ServerToolUseBlock and ServerToolResultBlock content types are parsed, handler stubs are replaced, and examples are updated

tasks.description: Refactor checkpoint.go control request helpers into reusable generics: extract sendControlRequest, awaitControlResponseTyped[T], buildControlTransportArgs, and startControlTransport from the existing RewindFiles implementation so new Client control methods can reuse them without duplication
tasks.commit: refactor(control): extract reusable control request helpers from checkpoint
steps: Step 1: Extract buildControlTransportArgs from buildRewindArgs (parameterize name) and generateControlRequestID from generateRequestID (parameterize prefix)
Step 2: Extract sendControlRequest as a generic sender replacing sendRewindFilesRequest -- accepts any payload struct
Step 3: Implement awaitControlResponseTyped[T] using Go generics that unmarshals the response JSON into type T, replacing awaitControlResponse which only checks success/error
Step 4: Extract startControlTransport helper that encapsulates client state checks (closed, busy, session), transport creation, Start, and sendInitializeRequest
Step 5: Rewrite RewindFiles to use the new generalized helpers, ensuring all existing checkpoint_test.go tests still pass
acceptance_criteria: All existing checkpoint_test.go tests pass without modification
RewindFiles uses the new shared helpers instead of private single-use functions
awaitControlResponseTyped correctly unmarshals typed responses and returns errors for error subtypes
startControlTransport returns ErrClientClosed, ErrClientBusy, ErrNoSession for appropriate states
go vet ./... passes clean

tasks.description: Add ServerToolUseBlock (type: server_tool_use) and ServerToolResultBlock (type: server_tool_result) content block types to content.go and wire them into parseContentBlocksFromSlice in parse.go. These represent MCP server tool invocations that the Python SDK supports but our parser currently silently drops.
tasks.commit: feat(content): add ServerToolUseBlock and ServerToolResultBlock types
steps: Step 1: Define ServerToolUseBlock struct in content.go with fields: Type string, ID string, Name string, Input json.RawMessage; implement BlockType() returning 'server_tool_use'
Step 2: Define ServerToolResultBlock struct in content.go with fields: Type string, ToolUseID string, Content json.RawMessage; implement BlockType() returning 'server_tool_result'
Step 3: Add cases for 'server_tool_use' and 'server_tool_result' in parseContentBlocksFromSlice switch in parse.go (around line 294), parsing fields from the blockMap
Step 4: Add unit tests in parse_test.go verifying both new block types are parsed correctly from JSON and that unknown types continue to be silently dropped
acceptance_criteria: ServerToolUseBlock and ServerToolResultBlock implement ContentBlock interface
parseContentBlocksFromSlice correctly parses server_tool_use and server_tool_result blocks from assistant message content
Unknown block types are still silently skipped (no regression)
Unit tests cover round-trip JSON -> ContentBlock for both new types
go test ./... passes

tasks.description: Add Client methods ReconnectMCPServer(ctx, serverName), ToggleMCPServer(ctx, serverName, enabled), and GetMCPStatus(ctx) using the generalized control request helpers from CASG-0014. These enable runtime MCP server management matching the Python SDK's reconnect_mcp_server, toggle_mcp_server, and get_mcp_status.
tasks.commit: feat(client): add MCP control methods (reconnect, toggle, status)
steps: Step 1: Implement ReconnectMCPServer(ctx context.Context, serverName string) error -- sends subtype 'mcp_reconnect' with serverName field via startControlTransport + sendControlRequest + awaitControlResponseTyped[struct{}]
Step 2: Implement ToggleMCPServer(ctx context.Context, serverName string, enabled bool) error -- sends subtype 'mcp_toggle' with serverName and enabled fields
Step 3: Implement GetMCPStatus(ctx context.Context) (*McpStatusResponse, error) -- sends subtype 'get_mcp_status' and returns parsed response via awaitControlResponseTyped[McpStatusResponse]

Step 4: Add unit tests using mock transport pattern from checkpoint_test.go: verify correct payloads sent, success/error responses handled, and precondition checks (no session, closed, busy) return appropriate errors
acceptance_criteria: ReconnectMCPServer sends correct control_request payload and handles success/error
ToggleMCPServer sends correct control_request with serverName and enabled fields
GetMCPStatus returns parsed McpStatusResponse on success
All three methods return ErrNoSession when no session is established
All three methods return ErrClientClosed and ErrClientBusy appropriately
go test ./... passes

tasks.description: Add Client methods GetContextUsage(ctx), GetServerInfo(ctx), and StopTask(ctx, taskID) using the generalized control request helpers. Define ServerInfoResponse type. These match the Python SDK's get_context_usage, get_server_info, and stop_task.
tasks.commit: feat(client): add context usage, server info, and stop task methods
steps: Step 1: Implement GetContextUsage(ctx context.Context) (*ContextUsageResponse, error) -- sends subtype 'get_context_usage' and returns parsed response via awaitControlResponseTyped[ContextUsageResponse]
Step 2: Define ServerInfoResponse struct in types.go (use map[string]any for flexibility since the CLI schema may evolve: Commands, OutputStyle, AvailableOutputStyles fields)
Step 3: Implement GetServerInfo(ctx context.Context) (*ServerInfoResponse, error) -- sends subtype 'get_server_info' and returns parsed response
Step 4: Implement StopTask(ctx context.Context, taskID string) error -- sends subtype 'stop_task' with task_id field, returns error only
Step 5: Add unit tests for all three methods following the mock transport pattern
acceptance_criteria: GetContextUsage returns parsed ContextUsageResponse on success
ServerInfoResponse type is defined in types.go
GetServerInfo returns parsed ServerInfoResponse on success
StopTask sends correct control_request with task_id field
All methods enforce session/closed/busy preconditions
go test ./... passes

tasks.description: Replace auto-ack stubs in control_handler.go for mcp_reconnect, mcp_toggle, and stop_task (currently line 34: blanket sendControlSuccess) with proper handler functions that parse request payloads and return structured responses
tasks.commit: feat(control): implement proper handlers for MCP and task control requests
steps: Step 1: Implement handleMcpReconnect that parses ControlMcpReconnectRequest and returns structured success response
Step 2: Implement handleMcpToggle that parses ControlMcpToggleRequest and returns structured success response
Step 3: Implement handleStopTask that parses ControlStopTaskRequest and returns structured success response
Step 4: Update the switch in handleControlRequest to call the new handlers instead of the blanket auto-ack
Step 5: Add unit tests verifying handlers parse payloads correctly and return expected responses, and that malformed JSON returns control error responses
acceptance_criteria: mcp_reconnect, mcp_toggle, and stop_task are handled by dedicated functions instead of blanket auto-ack
Each handler parses the typed request struct from ControlRequestMessage.Request
Malformed JSON payloads return control error responses instead of silent success
go test ./... passes

tasks.description: Update examples/streaming-mode/main.go with new examples demonstrating MCP status queries, context usage inspection, server tool content block handling, and the with_options example updated to use AllowedTools and Env matching the Python SDK's pattern
tasks.commit: docs(examples): add MCP control, server tools, and updated options examples
steps: Step 1: Add exampleMCPStatus function demonstrating GetMCPStatus and displaying server connection states
Step 2: Add exampleContextUsage function demonstrating GetContextUsage and displaying token breakdown
Step 3: Add exampleServerToolBlocks function showing how to handle ServerToolUseBlock and ServerToolResultBlock in message iteration via type assertions
Step 4: Update exampleWithOptions to use WithAllowedTools and WithEnv matching the Python SDK's with_options example pattern
Step 5: Register all new examples in the main() examples map and order slice
acceptance_criteria: go build ./examples/... compiles clean
New examples are registered and accessible via command-line name
Examples demonstrate type assertions for ServerToolUseBlock and ServerToolResultBlock
exampleWithOptions uses WithAllowedTools and WithEnv instead of just WithSystemPrompt
go vet ./examples/... passes
